name: Harold Jones
abstract: Harold Jones (born February 27, 1940) is an American traditional pop and jazz drummer who is best known as the drummer for Tony Bennett and for his five years with the Count Basie Orchestra. In a career spanning six decades, Jones has toured and recorded with Frank Sinatra, Duke Ellington, Oscar Peterson, Herbie Hancock, B.B.

about_quotes.speaker: Patricia Willard
about_quotes.evidence: in my group I had Jimmy Jones, piano, Harold Jones, drums, and Jim Hewitt, bass.

about_quotes.speaker: Mike Wofford
about_quotes.evidence: So I played with several different drummers... and then Harold Jones finished that first year... at that point it was still Andy and Harold Jones still.
about_quotes.doc_title: Mike Wofford interviewed by Monk Rowe, San Diego, California, February 13, 1998

about_quotes.speaker: Eddie Metz Jr.
about_quotes.evidence: So at one point we’re in California in San Francisco and Harold Jones, the drummer, shows up. He was with I think Natalie Cole at the time and they were on break. So he came to see the band, it was all his old buddies.
about_quotes.doc_title: Eddie Metz Jr. interviewed by Monk Rowe, New Hartford, New York, October 3, 2015

about_quotes.speaker: Butch Miles
about_quotes.evidence: I’ve talked with some of the other wonderful drummers that were in the band... with the exception of Harold Jones... all of us are still worried
about_quotes.doc_title: Butch Miles interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, October 9, 2020 [interview 2 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: Patricia Willard
about_quotes.evidence: The new one that Frank de la Rosa and Harold Jones have. ... And the new one, yeah, Right. They call themselves the Bossmen.

about_quotes.speaker: Louie Bellson
about_quotes.evidence: Today there’s a guy around called Harold Jones that’s another fabulous drummer. He lives up here. He can really play.
about_quotes.doc_title: Louie Bellson — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
